commands.review_summary:
The branch adds flat and nested channel identity fields to plugin agent hook contexts, exports nested SDK types, wires sender/chat IDs through reply and embedded runner hooks, and adds a sender-scoped session-key override.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +101, Docs +33, Generated 0. Total +258 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an additive Plugin SDK capability, not a current-main bug report. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the requested sender/chat hook context fields.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add extensible channel identity hook context
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an extensible `ctx.channel` hook context with `channel.sender.id` and `channel.chat.id` for channel-owned identity metadata.
- Preserves the existing flat `ctx.senderId` compatibility field and adds `ctx.chatId` so current hook consumers can keep working while plugins can module-augment nested channel metadata.
- Wires the identity context through reply/session creation and embedded runner hook contexts, with docs and focused regression coverage.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: plugin hooks can receive channel-scoped sender and chat identity in a structured extensible context while keeping the existing flat sender ID field.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout using the repo `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` wrapper after rebasing onto latest `upstream/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 3 Vitest shards passed; 117 tests passed.

Observed result after fix: hook context helper tests prove `channel.sender.id` mirrors `senderId` and `channel.chat.id` mirrors `chatId`; reply/session and embedded runner tests prove those fields are preserved through the runtime hook entry points.

What was not tested: no live external channel delivery was run in this public branch.
target.author: lanzhi-lee
